text:
In this paper we describe the ECCO version 4 (v4) non-linear inverse modeling framework and the resulting ECCO v4 estimate of the global ocean state for the period 1992–2011. The software framework and model result are designed to be fully transparent and highly reproducible. The development and model realization is verified to be stable and fully reproducible by using automated regression testing. The modeling framework employs several global conformal grids, a variety of data constraints and an algorithmic differentiation interface, built on a versatile, grid-independent Matlab toolbox.

The baseline ECCO v4 solution is dynamically consistent with the evolving state of the ocean while without residual unknowns such as unaccounted for heating or buoyancy. This version of the model has been tested against a wide range of observational data that confirmed that the solution is realistic and it results in a very good fit to the data in the overwhelming majority of cases. In ECCOv4 this model framework and the associated state estimate are presented in tandem for the first time; a crucial aspect of this formulation was the role of the inversion of process parameterizations in producing an optimal ocean hydrography. The resulting optimized parameters have been adopted into the standard ECCOv4 modeling framework.

We finally discuss the impact of structural, parametric and external model errors on the solution, and try to give some quantitative estimate on their respective weights. Our analysis shows that parametric and external errors are of the same order and both are typically larger than structural errors. This underlines the necessity of including turbulent transport parameters in the inversion process to have a good representation of the general circulation of the ocean.
